items.title: June 16, 2005: Detroit's Lindsey Hunter finishes with 17 PTS (7-10 FG)/5 AST off the bench during a 102-71 win over San Antonio in Game 4 of the NBA Finals.
items.author: NBA Cobwebs
items.body_excerpt:
June 16, 2005: Detroit's Lindsey Hunter finishes with 17 PTS (7-10 FG)/5 AST off the bench during a 102-71 win over San Antonio in Game 4 of the NBA Finals.

Chauncey Billups had 17 PTS/7 AST for the Pistons, who tied the series at 2-2. The Spurs' Tim Duncan had 16 PTS/16 REB.

items.title: Tiago Splitter was all class during his time in Portland, through to the very end.
items.author: Conor Bergin
items.body_excerpt:
Tiago Splitter was all class during his time in Portland, through to the very end.

He was the only non-player in the org to address media after the Billups arrest. Then he helped keep the season afloat through injuries to get the Blazers to the playoffs.

I'm happy he's getting this shot in Chicago

items.title: Not much news out of Dan Tolzman's news conference. Said NIL has made it harder to find diamonds in the rough at 19, a lot of bigs in the region they're picking, Webster has been as big of a part of process as ever.

items.body_excerpt: Not much news out of Dan Tolzman's news conference. Said NIL has made it harder to find diamonds in the rough at 19, a lot of bigs in the region they're picking, Webster has been as big of a part of process as ever.

items.title: Tiago Splitter thing I'm interested in: Blazers were 29th in mid-range frequency ... only behind Billy Donovan's Bulls. Caleb Wilson took a lot of non-rim twos at UNC. Feels like the midrange has to be part of Caleb's shot profile. Will Splitter let him cook there?
items.author: Ricky O'Donnell

items.title: 16 wins. Just 3 losses. 🤯
items.author: NBA
items.body_excerpt:
16 wins. Just 3 losses. 🤯

En route to their championship, the Knicks became just the 3rd team to go 16-3 or better in the Playoffs since 2003 (when the NBA expanded to best-of-seven in all rounds), joining the 2017 Warriors (16-1) and 2024 Celtics (16-3).
